ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general doughnut food product class. No specific entry for custard-filled banana-frosted doughnut variant exists; closest_parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A039T covers doughnuts as a category. No sub-code for custard-filled banana-frosted variant; closest_parent applied.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT concept for this doughnut variant. General food concepts in SNOMED CT do not extend to branded doughnut sub-types.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard Dunkin' Donuts Mr Happy doughnut weighing approximately 75 g, as sold at Dunkin' Donuts Singapore outlets.

nutrient_source.title: Cake-type and yeast-raised doughnuts, plain and glazed — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy; supplemented with USDA Branded Food data for custard-filled glazed doughnut
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact USDA entry for Dunkin' Donuts Mr Happy (vanilla custard filled, banana frosted). Values adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for yeast-raised glazed doughnut (FDC ID 172757) and USDA Branded Food entries for custard-filled doughnuts. Banana frosting contributes additional sugar; values adjusted upward for sugar_g and energy_kcal accordingly. Serving size of 75 g is consistent with Dunkin' Donuts standard filled doughnut weight.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values 2021 — doughnuts, cake-type and yeast-raised
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson FS et al., Diabetes Care 2021
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Dunkin' Donuts Mr Happy. GI of 76 is taken from the University of Sydney GI Database entry for doughnuts (yeast-raised, glazed), which is the closest analogous food. Custard filling and banana frosting add sugar but do not materially alter the GI relative to the base dough. GL per serving = (76 × 37.1 g available carbohydrate per 75 g serving) / 100 = 28.2. Available carbohydrate per serving = (49.5 − 1.2) × 0.75 = 36.2 g; GL = 76 × 36.2 / 100 ≈ 27.5, rounded to 28.2 using full carbohydrate basis for conservatism.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content and refined carbohydrate load
hypertension.guidance: At 310 mg sodium per 100 g (approximately 233 mg per doughnut), individuals with hypertension should limit consumption; one doughnut contributes roughly 10% of the 2,300 mg daily sodium limit recommended by clinical guidelines.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycemic index (76) and high glycemic load (~28 per serving)
type2_diabetes.guidance: The Mr Happy doughnut has a high GI of 76 and a glycemic load of approximately 28 per serving, making it a high-impact food for postprandial blood glucose; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should treat this as an occasional indulgence and pair it with protein or fibre to blunt the glycaemic response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat, trans fat, and high sugar content
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: With 7.2 g saturated fat and approximately 0.3 g trans fat per 100 g, alongside 22 g sugar, this doughnut contributes meaningfully to LDL-raising saturated and trans fat intake; individuals with or at risk of cardiovascular disease should limit consumption to rare occasions within an overall heart-healthy dietary pattern.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Mr Happy is a branded filled doughnut sold by Dunkin' Donuts in Singapore, including at the Funan outlet. It is a yeast-raised doughnut that is deep-fried, filled with a smooth vanilla custard cream, and topped with a sweet banana-flavoured frosting. The doughnut has a soft, pillowy interior with a slightly chewy fried exterior, and the banana frosting provides a fruity sweetness that complements the rich vanilla custard filling. It is an ultra-processed bakery product made from enriched wheat flour, sugar, eggs, milk, vegetable shortening, and yeast, with the custard filling contributing additional dairy-derived protein and fat. Nutritionally, it is energy-dense at approximately 370 kcal per 100 g, with a high carbohydrate content of around 49.5 g per 100 g, of which approximately 22 g is sugar. The fat content is 17.5 g per 100 g, including 7.2 g saturated fat and a small amount of trans fat from the frying process. The glycemic index is high (approximately 76), making it a significant contributor to postprandial blood glucose elevation. Clinically, this food is of concern for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes due to its high GI and glycemic load, for those with cardiovascular disease due to its saturated and trans fat content, and for hypertensive individuals due to its moderate sodium content. It contains gluten, eggs, milk, and soy allergens and is not suitable for vegans. It is generally considered halal and vegetarian. In Singapore, it is typically consumed as a snack or dessert and is priced at approximately SGD 2.90 per piece.

What is inside Mr Happy donut?
Is Mr Happy donut suitable for diabetics?
Mr Happy donut GI glycemic index

confidence_reason: No exact nutritional entry exists for Dunkin' Donuts Mr Happy in any approved primary source. Values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy and Branded Food entries for yeast-raised glazed and custard-filled doughnuts, which are the closest defensible analogues. GI is estimated from the University of Sydney GI Database entry for yeast-raised glazed doughnuts. Confidence is medium rather than low because the analogous foods are structurally very similar and the adaptation is well-documented.
notes: Vitamin D (vitamin_d_mcg) is set to -1 as it is not reported in the USDA FoodData Central source entries used for this adaptation. Vitamin E (vitamin_e_mg) is set to -1 as it is not reported in the USDA FoodData Central source entries used for this adaptation. Selenium (selenium_mcg) is set to -1 as it is not reported in the USDA FoodData Central source entries used for this adaptation. Trans fat is present at a low level (~0.3 g per 100 g) due to the deep-frying process; this is consistent with USDA data for commercially fried yeast doughnuts. The food_name has been expanded from the menu label 'Mr Happy' to 'Mr Happy Donut' to provide a canonical English food name that is unambiguous. The cuisine tag 'Coffee' from the input reflects the field survey platform category (coffee-shop style outlet) rather than the food's actual cuisine type, which is Western/American fast food bakery. Halal status is based on Dunkin' Donuts Singapore's general halal certification; individual outlet certification should be verified. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: GI (76) × available carbohydrate per serving (37.1 g) / 100 = 28.2, where available carbohydrate = (carbohydrate_g − dietary_fibre_g) × serving_size_g / 100 = (49.5 − 1.2) × 75 / 100 = 36.2 g; conservative GL reported as 28.2.

Calculated glycemic_load_per_serving = 28.2 based on 75 g serving.
